smtlib2:
(declare-fun F (Bool) Bool)
(declare-fun D () Bool)
(declare-fun C () Bool)
(declare-fun B () Bool)
(declare-fun A () Bool)
(assert (F (and (=> A B) (or C D))))
policy: All production deployments must satisfy the following requirements: if a deployment includes database schema changes, then it must have undergone integration testing in the staging environment. Additionally, either the deployment must include automated rollback procedures or it must be scheduled during a designated maintenance window.

test_cases.scenario: Gabriele Sacco from the platform team deployed version 2.4.1 to production on March 15th. The deployment included database schema changes to add new user preference columns, and the code had successfully passed all integration tests in staging on March 12th. The deployment included automated rollback procedures but was executed during normal business hours outside the maintenance window.

Edward Lambright released hotfix 1.3.2 on April 8th that modified the user authentication table structure. Due to the urgent nature of the security patch, the deployment bypassed the staging integration tests and went directly to production. However, the release included both automated rollback procedures and was scheduled during the Sunday 2 AM maintenance window.

Victoria Wang deployed a frontend-only update on May 3rd that contained no database changes. The deployment did not undergo staging integration testing since it only involved JavaScript and CSS modifications. The release included automated rollback capabilities but occurred during peak traffic hours at 11 AM on a Tuesday.
